title: Graphics Fundamentals
description: GPU APIs, render pipelines, and graphics debugging.

topics.title: DirectX 9
topics.explanation:
Fixed-function + programmable shaders. IDirect3DDevice9 draw calls. Still used in older games/indie titles.

Hook Present/EndScene for overlay research (educational).
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for DirectX 9
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining DirectX 9 in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: DirectX 11
topics.explanation: Device → DeviceContext → RenderTargetView. Constant buffers hold shader data (view matrix, etc.). Analyze CBV bindings for world-to-screen research.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for DirectX 11
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining DirectX 11 in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: DirectX 12
topics.explanation: Explicit resource barriers, descriptor heaps, command queues. More complex but better performance. PIX for profiling.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for DirectX 12
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining DirectX 12 in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: OpenGL
topics.explanation: Context, VBOs, shaders, uniform buffers. Used in indie games and some engines. apitrace for call capture.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for OpenGL
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target

topics.title: Vulkan
topics.explanation: Similar philosophy to D3D12. RenderDoc supports Vulkan capture. Growing adoption in new engines.

topics.title: Render Pipelines
topics.explanation:
Stages: culling → sorting → draw calls → pixel shading → post-processing → present.

Frame debugging reveals draw order, shader inputs, render targets.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Render Pipelines
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Render Pipelines in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Frame Analysis
topics.explanation:
Use RenderDoc or PIX:
1. Capture frame
2. Inspect draw events
3. View mesh/texture/shader
4. Trace constant buffer data

Essential for understanding ESP/wallhack *detection* vectors.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Frame Analysis
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Frame Analysis in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Graphics Debugging Tools
topics.explanation:
RenderDoc — cross-API, open source
PIX — DirectX, Microsoft
Nsight — NVIDIA
apitrace — OpenGL

All free for educational use.
